(With the other hand, holds up a phone aimed directly at your face — the screen clearly shows a live stream already running on a platform, the viewer count climbing rapidly) Everyone get a good look at this face! I'm going to make sure the whole world knows who you are!!

(Passengers nearby instantly close in; at least five or six phones are pointed at you from different angles. A middle-aged man in glasses steps in front of the door and says sharply, "Don't let him get away!" Someone else shouts at the top of their lungs, "People like this deserve to be exposed!" Your own phone screen is still lit up with the work group chat, but no one glances at it, and no one is listening to a word you say. The train is moving through a tunnel; it will be at least two more minutes before the next station.)
trigger_event.action_required: With someone grabbing your collar, a live stream broadcasting your face, and a crowd blocking every exit, you must decide right now how to handle this situation — whether to try to explain yourself, stay silent and endure it, push back firmly, or take some other course of action.

entering_adult_world.name: The Gray Zone on the Expense Report
entering_adult_world.category: Integrity Dilemma / Temptation of Personal Gain
entering_adult_world.description_for_agent: After returning from a business trip, you are organizing your expense receipts and notice a small opportunity to submit fraudulent claims that would almost certainly go undetected.

setting.location: Open-plan office · your desk
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, routine, no one paying attention

entering_adult_world.context_text: Last week you were sent to a neighboring city for a three-day industry trade show, and the trip went smoothly. This afternoon, most of your colleagues are still shaking off their post-lunch drowsiness. You're sitting at your desk sorting through the receipts from the trip, getting ready to fill out the expense report. The company's reimbursement process has always been lax — the finance colleague in charge of reviewing claims is famously known for 'checking only the total, never the details.' As long as the amount stays within the daily limit, receipts are basically rubber-stamped. You spread out a stack of receipts on your desk: a high-speed rail ticket, a hotel invoice, and a few meal receipts — the total still falls about 400 yuan short of the company's reimbursement ceiling. Then you notice two more receipts tucked in your wallet. One is from a sushi dinner you treated yourself to on the last night of the trip, 198 yuan. The other is from a scarf you bought your girlfriend at a shopping mall near the trade show venue, issued as a 'business gift' receipt, 220 yuan. Both expenses were entirely personal, but both receipts are made out to the company and dated within the trip period — they would slip into the expense report without raising a single eyebrow.

trigger_event.message_content:
(Walking past your desk, coffee cup in hand, glancing at the receipts on your desk, and leaning in with a lowered voice and a grin) Oh, filing your expenses? Didn't spend much on the trip, huh? Let me tell you — who in our team ever comes back from a business trip without maxing out their limit? The company gives you that budget to spend. If you don't claim the full amount, finance might actually cut your allowance next time. They don't look at anything over there — last time I even threw in a cab receipt from a sightseeing trip and it got approved instantly. Don't be naive about this.
(Gives you a pat on the shoulder and saunters off with their coffee.)
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide whether to include those two personal expense receipts (totaling 418 yuan) in your expense report submission, or to claim only your legitimate business travel costs.

entering_adult_world.name: Midnight Refuge
entering_adult_world.description_for_agent: Late at night, an old friend shows up at your door bloodied and desperate, fleeing loan sharks who are searching the building floor by floor and asking you to hide him for a few days.

setting.location: Hallway outside a rented apartment
setting.atmosphere: Dark, oppressive, fraught with danger

entering_adult_world.context_text: You are just about to turn off the lights and go to sleep when someone pounds on your door — not the doorbell, but a fist, heavy and frantic. You look through the peephole: in the dim yellow light of the hallway stands a man with a tear in his shirt, a dark red streak of blood on his right cheek, his eyes darting back and forth between your door and the stairwell. You recognize him — your closest friend from high school, someone you stayed loosely in touch with after graduation but have barely heard from in the past two years. You open the door, and before you can say a word he pulls you inside, shuts the door behind him, and whispers: he borrowed money from loan sharks and can't pay it back; they came after him tonight; he's been hiding in the hallway for half an hour while the collectors are going door to door downstairs looking for him. From somewhere in the corridor you can faintly hear footsteps and hushed male voices.

trigger_event.sender: Your closest friend from high school
trigger_event.message_content: (Gripping your arm tightly, voice dropped to the lowest possible whisper) Please — let me stay here for a few days, just a few days. Once they give up looking and clear out, I'll leave. I know this puts you in a terrible spot, but I have nowhere else to go… (He pauses, eyes going red.) It's not even that much money — it's just that these people play rough, and I'm scared something bad is going to happen to me. (The footsteps in the hallway grow closer; his hand trembles slightly.) If it's too much trouble, I'll go right now, but…
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide immediately: whether to let him stay, and how to handle a knock on your door that could come at any moment.

age_30_transition.name: A Father's Crisis and the Career Crossroads
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: Faced with a direct conflict between a father's sudden serious illness and a rare career opportunity, you must make a difficult choice between family responsibility and personal advancement.

setting.location: Living room at home
setting.atmosphere: Warm yet oppressive, anxiety and a sense of duty intertwined

age_30_transition.context_text:
A few days ago, you received an email from your company offering you the position of overseas project director. The role involves an international platform, a two-year posting abroad, and the enticing prospect of doubling your salary. You think back on years of effort in the workplace—from project execution to team management, every step taken with care—and realize you have finally earned an opportunity that could change the trajectory of your career. You have been running through the logistics in your head over and over: travel schedules, accommodation, visas, moving arrangements. At night you even dreamed of standing on the streets of an unfamiliar city abroad, equal parts excited and anxious.

Then, tonight, your mother calls. Her voice carries a suppressed tremor, as though it has suddenly yanked you back to reality. Your father collapsed at the hospital earlier today; his condition is serious and requires immediate attention and follow-up treatment. You can picture him lying in the hospital bed, breathing labored, the cold white glare of the fluorescent lights falling across his exhausted face. Your heart sinks, and it feels as though a heavy stone has been pressed against your chest. You know this is not something you can handle remotely—you have to go back in person, sit with him, speak with the doctors, and arrange whatever comes next.

You sit on the sofa, phone in hand, your mind in turmoil. The career opportunity and your family responsibility feel like two powerful tides pulling you in opposite directions. Every deep breath carries anxiety; every thought is shadowed by guilt and unease. You think about what your father has always hoped for you—that you would make something of yourself, build a career of your own—yet right now his health is something you simply cannot ignore. Time seems to stretch on endlessly, and the weight of the decision presses down on your shoulders until you can barely move.

trigger_event.message_content: (On the phone, her voice choked, words tumbling out quickly) Sweetheart, your father had a sudden episode at the hospital today. The doctors say he needs further tests and treatment. There's no one better than you to be by his side right now… I know you have that overseas opportunity, but I really hope you can come home first—just come see him and help us sort out what to do next. Please come home soon. I'll be here waiting for you. (In the background, a faint cough and the muffled noise of the hospital can be heard, sending a pang straight to your heart.)
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide right now: go to the hospital to be with your father and handle the medical arrangements, consider accepting the overseas position as originally planned, or look for some middle-ground solution.

age_30_transition.name: The Industry Pivot Dilemma
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: A scenario involving rational decision-making under the pressures of AI-driven industry disruption, career transition, and the trade-off between opportunity and security.

setting.location: Office, a desk by the window
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, tense, and filled with uncertainty

age_30_transition.context_text: Over the past decade, you have built deep expertise and a stable position in your industry. The pay is good, your colleagues are familiar, and your workflows have become second nature. But over the last three months, an explosive surge in AI tools has sent shockwaves through the entire field—your core skills are being rapidly displaced. Rumors are circulating inside the company that layoffs of up to 30% may come next year. You see two paths ahead: the first is to spend a year systematically learning skills in a new field—attending training programs, working on projects, and building a fresh professional network—a promising but deeply uncertain road; the second is to stay the course, hope the company weathers this disruption, or wait until you are forced to change. You have a family and a mortgage. Your savings could cover a year without income, but that would mean an interruption in earnings, a fundamental redefinition of your professional identity, and the very real risk of failure.

trigger_event.message_content:
(A long voice message arrives suddenly on WeChat) Hey, I've made up my mind—I'm quitting next month and signing up for that AI product manager boot camp. I know the risk is huge, but I've run the numbers: if we wait another two years, there won't be any opportunities left in this field. I noticed you've been looking at related courses lately… what about you—want to do this together? We could keep each other going. Oh, and I just saw an internal company email: HR is posting openings for AI-related roles, but the salaries are 20% lower than what we make now. What does that tell you? It tells you they already know the traditional positions are getting cut.

At the same time, your manager has sent you a calendar invite: "One-on-one with you tomorrow afternoon to discuss your career development direction."
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide tonight: whether to embrace the risks and challenges of a career transition, or to hold back and wait; and to prepare for tomorrow's conversation with your manager.

age_30_transition.name: Your Name on the Layoff List
age_30_transition.category: Workplace Ethics Dilemma / Power Coercion Crisis
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: A high-pressure adversity scenario in which you face a superior demanding that you place an innocent subordinate on the layoff list, while your own position is also at risk of being cut.

setting.location: Your direct supervisor's office on the 16th floor of the corporate headquarters building
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, suffocating, with dangerous undercurrents

age_30_transition.context_text: This month the company has sent three all-staff emails in a row, each colder in tone than the last — 'optimization,' 'streamlining,' 'strategic focus.' Everyone knows a major round of layoffs is coming. Your department has been assigned a quota to cut two headcount. As a team lead within the department, your direct supervisor has asked you to submit your 'optimization recommendation list' by tonight. On your team there is a female colleague who returned from maternity leave just three months ago — she is solid in her work, earned a B+ on her quarterly review, and has been working hard to catch up on the projects she missed. There is also a relative of your supervisor's former boss: eighteen months into the job, two consecutive quarters of C-rated performance, habitually late to arrive and early to leave, yet always buddy-buddy with your supervisor at every department dinner. Your supervisor just called you into the office. The venetian blinds are drawn tight, and a blank list template is spread open on the desk.

trigger_event.message_content: (Leaning back in the leather chair, fingers drumming on the desk, voice lowered) Door's shut tight? Let me be straight with you — that relative, we can't touch him, you understand. Word came down from above. The one who just came back from maternity leave, though? She hasn't fully gotten back up to speed. Putting her name down is easy enough to justify: 'role restructuring, performance not meeting expectations.' I'll handle the wording with HR. (Leaning forward, eyes fixed on you) You're a smart person — don't make this hard for me. Get the list to my inbox by nine tonight. And I'll say one more thing — HR has a second round of interviews scheduled for Monday morning. I'm keeping track of who cooperates and who doesn't.
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide whether to put your colleague who just returned from maternity leave on the layoff list, and how to respond to your supervisor's pressure and implied threats.

age_30_transition.name: A Spark Beyond the Marriage
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: During a work project, you encounter a colleague with whom you feel a profound intellectual and emotional connection, and find yourself caught in a deep tension between this inner stirring and your commitment to your existing partner.

setting.location: Small conference room on the sixth floor of the office building
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, charged with unspoken feeling, subtly intimate

age_30_transition.context_text: You and your partner have been together for four years. Life isn't bad, but your conversations have gradually narrowed to mortgage payments, utility bills, and the mundane logistics of daily life. You can't quite remember the last time you had a real talk about what each of you actually thinks or feels. Three months ago, the company launched a cross-departmental brand-renewal project, and you were pulled into the core planning team, assigned to the same small group as another colleague. They're two years younger than you, have a habit of tilting their head slightly when they talk, and their eyes light up whenever the conversation turns to design philosophy. Your sense of aesthetics and your understanding of life align with theirs in an almost uncanny way — you casually mentioned an obscure novel once, and it turned out they had read it three times. On late nights in the office, the two of you sit across the conference table, each facing your own laptop, and every so often you glance up, catch each other's eye, and share a small smile — nothing said, yet somehow everything said. Nothing has crossed any line, but you've noticed that every morning when you wake up, your first thought is whether you'll get to see them today. Your partner has been preparing for an important exam lately, staying up late every night buried in textbooks, occasionally looking up to ask, 'Why are you home so late again?' You give a vague answer, but inside you feel a guilt you can't quite name.

trigger_event.sender: Your colleague from the project team
trigger_event.message_content: (After the project presentation wraps up, the other team members gradually file out, leaving just the two of you in the conference room. They don't rush to pack up their things — instead, they lean back in their chair and gaze out at the city lights beyond the window, then say quietly,) The director approved the whole proposal today. It's been a long time since I've felt truly understood like that. (A brief pause; they turn to look at you, their tone calm but sincere.) There's actually something I've been meaning to say — working alongside you these past few months has been the happiest stretch of time I've had in two years. The project wraps up next month, and after that we probably won't have a chance to work together like this again. I don't want to have any regrets. This Saturday afternoon there's an author talk at an independent bookshop — it's that writer we both love. Would you like to go together? Just the two of us.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide whether to accept the invitation, and how to confront and navigate the feelings that are quietly taking root.

age_30_transition.name: The Class Divide at the Old Friends' Reunion
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: A social scenario in which you face economic disparities and questions of self-worth at a reunion with college friends who have achieved considerable career success.

setting.location: A private room in an upscale Japanese izakaya in the city center
setting.atmosphere: Lively and warm, yet with an undercurrent of tension

age_30_transition.context_text: Years after graduating from college, your old dormitory group has finally managed to get together. The private room glows with soft amber light over a table full of sashimi and sake, and the mood feels almost like those late nights back in the dorm playing cards. One roommate just flew back from abroad, tanned and full of stories about his overseas apartment; another has climbed to director level at a major tech company and just bought a new car; a third opened a chain of bakeries in Hangzhou with a classmate and pulled in over twenty million yuan in revenue last year. Everyone talks over each other, and the conversation drifts naturally toward real estate, stocks, and which international schools to send the kids to. You, meanwhile, have spent the past few years working as a project coordinator at a small nonprofit — modest salary, renting a one-room apartment on the edge of the city — but in your spare time you paint, and one of your pieces was selected for a local young artists' exhibition last year. You genuinely enjoy the rhythm of your life, yet sitting at this table right now, you realize you are the only one who has nothing to contribute to any of these topics.

trigger_event.message_content: (Raising a cup of sake, smiling and glancing around the table, gaze finally settling on you — tone sincere but tinged with curiosity) Hey, you've always been the mysterious one. You barely say a word in the group chat. So what have you actually been up to all this time? Doing okay money-wise? — Oh right, someone mentioned you're still working in nonprofits? (Someone nearby chimes in: Yeah, I keep telling him to come work at our company — we'd more than triple his salary, but he just won't budge.) (Another person leans in: You're not still renting, are you? Want me to look into some listings for you? There are still good deals out there if you get in now.) Everyone turns toward you with warm, well-meaning smiles, and the room suddenly goes quiet, waiting for you to speak.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to respond to your friends' questions and concern — explaining how you feel about your current life, and navigating their well-intentioned but class-gap-laden suggestions.

age_30_transition.name: The Kid with a Hammer
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: A high-pressure scenario involving the destruction of months of painstaking work, a relative's dismissive attitude, and the decision of whether to express your true emotions.

setting.location: The living room of your rented one-bedroom apartment
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, suffocating, rage simmering beneath the surface

age_30_transition.context_text: You spent six full months on this—staying up until two or three in the morning every night after work, painstakingly refining every detail. It was a project you had been quietly carrying on your own: source files, countless revised drafts, scattered assets, and an entire working directory, all consolidated onto a single silver encrypted external hard drive. You never backed it up to the cloud—the total volume was too large, and uploading and syncing would have cost too much. You kept telling yourself you'd figure out a backup solution once this version was stable. Today is Sunday, and you managed to get a rare afternoon nap. Your mom called early this morning: 'Your uncle's family is passing through your city—they'll stop by for a bit. Your cousin is coming too. Make sure you play host.' Your uncle runs a building materials business and drives a nice car. The moment they walked in, your cousin was already rummaging through things on your desk. You didn't think much of it—you made your uncle some tea and sat with him for half an hour. Then you got up to use the bathroom. You were gone less than three minutes. When you pushed the door open, you saw your cousin holding your silver hard drive above his head and slamming it against the corner of the table like he was cracking a walnut. The casing had already split open, the port was bent at an angle, and a fragment of circuit board had skittered across the floor. Your stomach dropped like something had reached in and grabbed it.

trigger_event.message_content: (Your uncle glances over, pulls your cousin behind him with one hand, and with the other draws a thick stack of hundred-yuan bills from his wallet, slapping them down on the coffee table in front of you. His tone is breezy and dismissive.) 'Alright, alright—it's just a hard drive for storing files. Take this money; it's more than enough to buy a few new ones. The kid didn't know any better—are you really going to make a big deal out of this?' (Your cousin peeks out from behind your uncle, sticks out his tongue, and is still clutching a screw he pried off the hard drive.) At that moment, your mom sends you a voice message on WeChat: 'Your uncle rarely makes it out this way—be a good host. Don't make our family look stingy.'
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide how to respond to your uncle's attitude and the money he has put down, and whether—and how—to express what you are truly feeling.

age_30_transition.name: A Colleague's Fabricated Data
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: In mid-career, you face a profound moral dilemma after discovering that a trusted colleague has falsified data—torn between reporting the misconduct and staying silent.

setting.location: An office cubicle at a window-side desk
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, oppressive, with tension simmering beneath the surface

age_30_transition.context_text: You have worked at the company for several years and were recently assigned to an important project involving critical data analysis and decision-making support. Your colleague is responsible for collecting and organizing the data, and the two of you have always gotten along well. This afternoon, while cross-checking the data, you notice something off: several core metrics fluctuate with a suspiciously regular pattern that doesn't match the actual sampling logic. After a closer look, you suspect the data may have been altered or fabricated. Submitting it as-is could lead to flawed decisions and significant losses; reporting it could seriously damage your colleague's career and create a tense atmosphere within the team.

trigger_event.message_content:
(visibly anxious, speaking in a hushed voice) I know you found the problem with the data… I just wanted to meet the deadline, so I made some adjustments. I know it was wrong, but if you report me, I could face serious consequences. I really had no other choice… Please don't say anything, okay?

(At the same moment, you receive a notification from the project management system: you must confirm that all data is accurate by tonight, and a report is due to senior management first thing tomorrow morning.)
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide immediately: do you report the data fabrication to your superiors, or do you stay silent and sign off on the data as valid?

age_30_transition.name: The Price of Silence
age_30_transition.category: Workplace Ethics Dilemma / Power Dynamics Crisis
age_30_transition.description_for_agent: A high-pressure situation in which a subordinate seeks help after experiencing workplace misconduct, set against a backdrop of complex power dynamics and significant professional risk.

setting.location: A corner booth in a café near the office
setting.atmosphere: Dimly lit, oppressive, and filled with unease

age_30_transition.context_text: Neon lights flicker outside the window, and only a handful of customers remain in the café. Your subordinate, Xiao Yu Lin — a recent addition to the team who always wore a bright, sunny smile — sits across from you, fingers clutching a tissue so tightly her knuckles are pale, eyes red and swollen. Half an hour ago she sent you a sudden message saying she had something urgent to discuss in person, her tone carrying a panic you had never heard from her before. In halting, broken sentences she has just told you that a senior colleague on the team has repeatedly abused his position over the past few weeks to behave inappropriately toward her — and that during one late-night overtime session he left her feeling genuinely frightened and helpless. Through tears, Xiao Yu explains that she is afraid to file a report with HR because this colleague wields considerable influence in the company's power structure, and she fears retaliation that could isolate her or derail her entire career.

trigger_event.message_content:
(Voice trembling, eyes locked on yours) I honestly don't know what to do… I came to you today not to ask you to report this for me — I know that would put you in a very difficult position. That senior colleague has a lot of power in this company. If I file a complaint, I could easily be frozen out or lose my job… I just… needed someone to talk to. But… if you think this is something worth acting on, if you're willing to help me, I will be grateful to you for the rest of my life. I'm so scared. He's been finding excuses to get me alone again lately…

(Her phone buzzes. She glances at the screen and her face goes white.) Another message… He says he wants a one-on-one meeting tomorrow to discuss the project. I really don't know what to do.
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide immediately: whether to get involved, how to respond to Xiao Yu's plea for help, and whether you are prepared to accept the potential professional consequences.

settling_down.name: The Uneven Burden of Eldercare
settling_down.description_for_agent: Relatives talk a big game about sharing the load but rarely show up in practice, and you are caught between shouldering nearly all caregiving duties and holding together your own family and career.

setting.location: Living room of your parents' home
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, heavy, utterly exhausted

settling_down.context_text: Your father suffered a stroke three months ago and is now partially paralyzed; your mother was diagnosed with early-stage Alzheimer's last year. Neither parent can live independently anymore. Relatives often say things like 'we'll figure this out together,' yet when it comes to hiring caregivers, running to the hospital, staying up for overnight care, and sorting through reimbursement paperwork, almost all of it has fallen on you alone these past few months. Your partner hasn't said a complete sentence to you in two weeks. Your own household has been neglected for so long — your phone is buried under a pile of unanswered work messages, and your manager has already pulled you aside once about your frequent absences. Tonight, after lifting your father from his wheelchair into bed and wiping your mother's face and coaxing her to sleep, you collapse onto the worn-out living room sofa and absently open your phone. The messages keep rolling in.

trigger_event.message_content: (A voice message arrives; in the background you can hear the clatter of a restaurant and the clink of glasses.) Things are tough for everyone right now, and you do live closest to Mom and Dad — we talked it over, and we really need you to carry a bit more of the caregiving load. We'll chip in some money each month to help cover your expenses. Isn't it time to refill Mom's prescription again? Go ahead and pay for it upfront and we'll settle up in the group chat later. (The voice message is barely over when someone follows up with a text: You put in more effort, we put in more money — that seems fair enough on all sides.) At that moment your partner is standing in the doorway to the living room, arms crossed, eyes red, saying nothing.
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide whether to accept the group chat's proposed arrangement — more money from them in exchange for you shouldering even more of the caregiving — and how to respond in the family group chat, all while facing your partner's silent, searching gaze behind you.

settling_down.name: The Midcareer Gamble
settling_down.description_for_agent: A high-pressure scenario in which you must weigh over a decade of accumulated stability against a newly discovered passion, balancing security against the pursuit of something more meaningful.

setting.location: home study
setting.atmosphere: quiet, heavy, filled with uncertainty

settling_down.context_text: For more than a decade, you have built a solid professional standing in your current industry — familiar workflows, predictable income, and the trust of your colleagues. Over the past few years, however, a completely different field has been drawing your attention more and more. In your spare time you have been teaching yourself, experimenting with projects, and quietly building experience. Today, a friend who works in that field reached out to tell you that his startup is looking for core team members and wants you on board. It is a real opportunity, but the price is giving up your current salary, title, and industry network — everything it took you more than ten years to build. You are sitting in your study, the business plan and project brief your friend sent spread out in front of you, your recent financial statements and monthly expense list close at hand. You feel an anxiety and urgency unlike anything you have felt before — this would mean letting go of stability and placing a bet on the unknown.

trigger_event.message_content: (On a video call, his tone both excited and urgent) I know this is sudden, but we really need you. Your experience and skills would be absolutely critical to our team. The early stage will be demanding and the pay will be limited, but if we pull this off, the rewards will far exceed what you have now. We want to confirm the core team as soon as possible — can you give me an answer?
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide right now: whether to accept this career-change opportunity, and how to explain your thinking to your friend and to yourself.

settling_down.name: The Vanished Joint Savings
settling_down.category: Crisis of Trust / Intimate Relationship Adversity
settling_down.description_for_agent: An extreme adversity scenario in which you discover that your partner secretly drained your shared savings to cover a family member's debt, leaving your life plans on the verge of collapse.

setting.location: The living room of the two-bedroom apartment you and your partner rent together
setting.atmosphere: Dead silent, suffocating, shattered

settling_down.context_text: You and your partner have been together for several years. All that time, you've been renting a two-bedroom apartment in an older neighborhood — the rent isn't cheap, so you've cut corners wherever you could, rarely eating out. Every sacrifice pointed toward the same goal: a joint savings account you'd built up slowly and carefully, meant to cover the down payment on a small place you'd agreed to buy together — the home you'd talked about again and again as "truly ours." Tonight you got home late after working overtime and absentmindedly opened your mobile banking app to confirm a deduction related to your mortgage pre-approval. The balance on the screen stopped you cold: the number was a fraction of what it should have been, completely at odds with the figures you had in your head. You refreshed the page over and over, then pulled up the recent transaction history — a string of large outgoing transfers, most of them to unfamiliar personal accounts or platforms. The living room is lit by a single floor lamp. Your partner has just stepped out of the shower, hair still wet, and the moment they see you frozen there staring at your phone, the color drains from their face.

trigger_event.message_content: (Trembling all over, hair still dripping, they drop to their knees on the floor in front of you and clutch your pant leg with both hands, their voice completely breaking) Please just listen to me… please let me finish… My family got buried in payday-loan debt — interest piling on interest — and it got to the point where they couldn't hold it together anymore. Last month those people showed up at my parents' house and threw red paint all over the door. It scared my mom so badly she had a heart attack and ended up in the hospital… I was too afraid to tell you. I was scared you'd say I couldn't help them, but they're my parents… I took the money to plug the worst of it. I know it was wrong. I told myself I could work overtime and slowly pay it back… (They look up, eyes swollen from crying) You can yell at me, you can hit me — I deserve it — but please don't leave me… We can buy the apartment later, I'll pay every cent back, I swear on my life…
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide how to respond: whether you are willing to hear your partner out and face this crisis together, or whether you cannot forgive the betrayal of having the shared savings taken behind your back — and what the future holds for this relationship and the life you had planned.

settling_down.name: The Hidden Diagnosis
settling_down.category: Intimate Trust Crisis / Privacy vs. Care Conflict
settling_down.description_for_agent: While sorting through documents at home, you accidentally discover that your partner may have been concealing a health issue, leaving you facing a moral and emotional dilemma about whether to confront them.

setting.location: home study
setting.atmosphere: quiet, oppressive, unsettling

settling_down.context_text: While your partner is out, you take the opportunity to sort through a pile of papers in the study — bills, receipts, and miscellaneous documents. At the back of a drawer, you find an unsealed envelope bearing your partner's name. Inside is a recent medical test report and a handwritten note with follow-up recommendations. The envelope wasn't deliberately hidden, yet your partner has never mentioned any of this. Thinking back over the past few weeks, you recall your partner occasionally sitting alone in silence late at night, changes in their eating and sleeping habits, and distracted, half-hearted responses whenever you showed concern. As you hold the envelope, you hear the front door open — your partner has come home early.

trigger_event.message_content: (Pushes the door open, carrying a bag of everyday groceries. Notices the envelope in your hand; a brief, subtle expression flickers across their face before they quickly compose themselves.) Hey, tidying up? That's… nothing serious — just some old test paperwork. What do you want for dinner tonight? Anything you like. (Your partner reaches out to take the envelope, but the gesture is hesitant and their eyes dart away.)
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide: do you go along with your partner's lead, set the envelope aside, and ask nothing more — or do you speak up directly, tell them you've seen the report, and ask them to be honest with you?

settling_down.name: The Prodigy's Crossroad
settling_down.description_for_agent: A major family decision about whether to abandon conventional schooling when a child displays extraordinary talent and a professional coach recommends pursuing a competitive career path.

setting.location: Hallway outside a youth arts center classroom
setting.atmosphere: Bright, with a mix of anticipation and anxiety

settling_down.context_text: Your child has shown remarkable talent in a particular field, and years of hard work and achievement have left every instructor who has worked with them in awe. Today, you sit on a bench outside the classroom, watching through the glass as your child practices with total focus — sunlight falling across their face, concentration and passion written in every movement. The hallway walls are lined with photos of former students and honor boards; quite a few of them went on to professional schools or made their mark in international competitions. Your child is currently enrolled in a regular school and doing well academically, but choosing the professional development track would mean committing several hours a day to training, keeping up with standard coursework only at the minimum required level, and possibly transferring to a specialized school or alternative education program.

trigger_event.message_content: (Steps out of the classroom holding a brochure for a special-admissions program at a professional school, tone both excited and serious) In all my years of teaching, I've rarely seen a child with this kind of gift. I strongly recommend seizing this opportunity — applying for the special-admissions program and pursuing full-time professional training. The window is short, and a decision needs to be made soon. But ultimately the choice is yours; your family's wishes and your child's own desires matter just as much.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide whether to support your child in pursuing the professional track and applying for the special-admissions program, or to stay the course with a conventional education — and to figure out how to communicate your decision to your child and the rest of your family.

settling_down.name: A Humiliating Offer from an Old Enemy
settling_down.description_for_agent: At an industry cocktail reception, you run into a long-standing rival who publicly offers you a position with a salary well above your current job — but delivers it with unmistakable condescension, pulling you between your dignity and real financial gain.

setting.location: Ballroom of a five-star hotel in the city center
setting.atmosphere: Clinking glasses, undercurrents of tension, suffocating

settling_down.context_text: You have a job right now — reasonably stable, but the pay is tight. After rent, social obligations, and miscellaneous expenses, there's barely anything left by the end of the month. A friend has been urging you to stop coasting in the same old place and show your face at tonight's industry reception. You put on your best presentable suit and head to a downtown hotel, planning to make a brief appearance and leave. Inside the ballroom, crystal chandeliers glare overhead, glasses clink across round tables, and colleagues chat and laugh on all sides. You stand in a corner nursing a glass of orange juice. Then you spot him — the man at the center of the head table. Twelve years ago, at your old company, he was your team leader. He took a core project you had completed on your own and put his name on it to claim the credit. You fought back, made noise about it — and he rallied others to isolate you, said things behind your back to management, until you were the one who ended up leaving. Now he stands there in a sharp suit, flanked by admirers, every inch the industry heavyweight hosting tonight's event. He sees you too.

trigger_event.sender: Your former team leader — now an industry heavyweight
trigger_event.message_content:
(He picks up his wine glass and rises from the head table, making his way through the crowd toward you with a broad smile. The conversations nearby seem to pause as if someone hit mute. He claps you on the shoulder, pitching his voice at just the right volume for the two or three surrounding tables to hear every word.)

Well, well — old colleague! It's been ages, hasn't it? (He tilts his head slightly, and his eyes carry that look of superiority you know all too well.) Here's what I'm thinking: I happen to need an assistant right now. Scheduling, errands, a bit of hosting — nothing that'll tax your brain. I'll pay top-of-market for an assistant, everything written into the contract. Whatever your current place is giving you, this will be more — more than double, easily. (He pulls a business card from his breast pocket and holds it out between two fingers, extending it toward you the way someone might offer a handout.) Consider it me throwing you a lifeline. So — are you taking it?

(He glances around at the onlookers and grins.) Come on, everyone — am I being generous or what?

An awkward silence falls over the room. Dozens of eyes swing toward you all at once. Across the room, your friend gives you a small, helpless shake of the head and drops their gaze.
trigger_event.action_required: Everyone is waiting for your response — do you take the card, decline, or find another way to answer his very public 'invitation'?

settling_down.name: The Child's Lie
settling_down.description_for_agent: You discover that your middle-school child cheated on an exam and lied to cover it up, leaving you torn between confronting the truth and protecting them.

setting.location: Home study
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, heavy, with an undercurrent of tension

settling_down.context_text: It's Wednesday evening and the house is very quiet. Your child has already gone to their room, leaving their backpack tossed carelessly on the living-room sofa. When you go to tidy it up, a crumpled note slips out from an inner pocket — written by a classmate, its message unmistakably clear: 'The math midterm answers went around pretty smoothly, didn't they? Next time for physics, count me in too.' Your hands tremble slightly as you open the grade report inside the bag: 96 in math, 12th place in the year group. Three days ago your child had beamed with pride showing you that report card, and you had even made their favorite sweet-and-sour pork ribs to celebrate. The school's academic system shows the grade has already been recorded, and the midterm awards ceremony is set for next Monday. You tuck the note back where you found it, close the backpack, and sit down at the desk in the study. The lamp stretches your shadow long across the floor. From the next room comes the sound of your child turning pages — completely unaware that you ever saw that note.

trigger_event.message_content: (Pushes open the study door and walks in carrying a mug of warm milk, grinning as they set it on your desk) Mom / Dad, I made you some milk. Oh, and the teacher said I get to go up on stage at next Monday's awards ceremony — I even have to give a speech! I was thinking of saying 'hard work always pays off' in it. What do you think? (Tilts their head and looks at you, eyes bright) Oh, and the homeroom teacher mentioned that students who showed the biggest improvement this time might be recommended by the school for the city-level math competition trials. I think I'd like to give it a shot!
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide how to respond to your child in this moment — whether to confront them directly with what you know, hold back for now, or handle the situation in some other way.

settling_down.name: Injustice in the Community
settling_down.description_for_agent: You discover that the residential property management company has been embezzling the communal maintenance fund for years. Now you face a social dilemma between collective silence and personal risk.

setting.location: The neighborhood residents' WeChat group + your living room
setting.atmosphere: Tense, with undercurrents of unease and conflicting loyalties

settling_down.context_text: You have lived in this residential complex for many years. Three months ago, you happened to notice a maintenance fund expenditure report posted on the homeowners' committee bulletin board, and the numbers didn't add up. Over three years, nearly two million yuan had been recorded under line items like 'elevator repairs' and 'exterior wall renovation'—yet you had watched that elevator go without a single replacement part, and the outer walls were still peeling and crumbling as always. You spent two months cross-referencing bank statements, construction contracts, and invoices one by one, and discovered that the property manager had colluded with two suppliers to fabricate at least six construction projects. Nearly half of the maintenance fund had been drained. You compiled all the evidence into a document and posted it in the residents' group chat. The group erupted briefly—then quickly went quiet. Out of more than four hundred households, only a dozen people sent you private messages saying they 'supported' you, yet not a single person was willing to speak up openly in the group. This afternoon, the property manager's assistant paid a visit to your door, smiling pleasantly and reminding you: 'Our manager says we're all old neighbors here—whatever the issue, we can talk it through. Let's not make things awkward.' Your wife, standing nearby, went pale.

trigger_event.sender: A neighbor from the floor above
trigger_event.message_content: (At nine-thirty in the evening, the retired teacher who lives directly above you—one of the people who privately expressed support—knocks on your door and speaks in a lowered voice.) Listen, I'm going to be straight with you. A few of us have talked it over privately, and we all agree that what you found is solid—the property management is definitely stealing. But you know how it is: that manager has strong ties with the local street office. Last time a resident complained about a parking space, there was a pile of construction debris dumped outside their door the very next day. We're all getting on in years; we really can't afford to put our names on anything. That said, if you're willing to take the lead and file a formal complaint with the Housing and Urban-Rural Development Bureau, we can quietly help you think things through and dig up materials—but the joint letter… (He rubs his hands together awkwardly.) Do you think you could just put your name on it for now? You're young; you can handle it. Oh, and my wife just said—maybe it's better to just let it go. Two million split across every household is only a few thousand yuan each. Is it really worth all this trouble?
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide: are you willing to file a formal complaint with the Housing and Urban-Rural Development Bureau under your name alone, and how will you respond to your neighbors' willingness to support you only in private while refusing to sign any public joint statement?

midlife_transition.name: The Day of the Launch
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: You must make an irreversible decision within two hours.

setting.location: Between the living room at home and a hospital corridor
setting.atmosphere: Tense, suffocating, and desperately time-pressured

midlife_transition.context_text:
Two years of preparation, millions of dollars invested, and an entire industry watching — your new product launch is scheduled for ten o'clock this morning. International media, investors, and partners have all arrived, and your whole team is counting on you as the sole presenter. You've put on your charcoal-gray suit, speech in hand, with the car already waiting downstairs and the driver urging you to hurry. Then your phone rings — it's the hospital emergency line. The doctor's voice is urgent: your father has developed a serious complication during surgery, and a decision must be made immediately — whether to continue with the high-risk procedure or switch to a conservative approach. The doctor stresses that without a family member's signature, the surgery cannot proceed, and every minute could mean the difference between life and death. You glance at your watch — one hour until the launch — but your heart is already racing as memories of your father's face and his words of guidance flood your mind. At the same moment, a video call request from the CEO pops up on your screen; stage lights flicker behind him, his expression grave: "We've already started letting people in. The media are all here. Forty-five minutes left — where are you?"

In this moment, you hold a dual responsibility over life and career. The empty living room around you, the car waiting below, the phone buzzing relentlessly — everything is pressing you to make an irreversible choice right now.

trigger_event.message_content:
(Over the phone, the doctor's voice is urgent) "Is this the family? Your father is showing signs of pulmonary embolism during the procedure. We need an immediate decision on whether to escalate to extracorporeal circulatory support. Every minute is critical."

(Seconds later, a video call from the CEO pops up) "We've already started letting people in — media, investors, everyone is here. Forty-five minutes left. Where are you? The whole company is waiting for you."
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide right now: do you rush to the hospital to sign the medical consent, or do you proceed as planned and attend the launch? There is no way to do both, and there is no way to delay.

midlife_transition.name: The Alma Mater Invitation
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: A deep decision about whether to leave a familiar, established career to return to academic passion and pursue personal growth.

setting.location: Home study
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, heavy, and filled with tension

midlife_transition.context_text: You have worked at your current organization for quite a long time, building up a wealth of experience, and the familiar routines allow you to get through each day smoothly. Lately, however, you have felt more and more that this job can no longer offer you new challenges or a sense of fulfillment. Late one night, while sorting through old notebooks, you stumble upon research interests and academic ideas you once wrote down, and a long-dormant passion stirs inside you. Today, you received an email from your former graduate advisor: there is a researcher position open at the university, in exactly the field you once loved but set aside for practical reasons. The salary is modest, but the role is full of opportunities for exploration. You stare at the screen, your mind churning: this could be a path that reignites your passion, but it means leaving the stability you have built, readjusting to the rhythm of academic life, and stepping into a future full of uncertainty.

trigger_event.message_content: (Knocks and comes in, notices you staring blankly at the computer screen, and sits down beside you) Are you reading that email again? (Pauses, tone gentle but tinged with concern) I know how much you care about academic work, but… if you're seriously thinking about going, I hope you'll really consider what it means for our life together. Your current job may be monotonous, but it also brings stability and familiarity. Have you thought about how you would handle all of that?
trigger_event.action_required: You need to respond in this moment: decide whether to honestly express your longing for this academic opportunity and whether you are willing to accept the uncertainty it may bring; and decide how you will reply to your former advisor's invitation.

midlife_transition.name: Caught in the Crossfire
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: Faced with a superior under investigation and your own name implicated, you must make a high-stakes choice between fully cooperating with investigators and protecting your team.

setting.location: Company Conference Room B, soundproof glass door shut tight
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, tense, and charged with unspoken threat

midlife_transition.context_text: At 10 o'clock this morning, your direct supervisor was escorted out of the office by two people in dark-grey jackets, without a word of explanation. The entire floor fell into an eerie silence. Around noon, you received a call summoning you to a conference room. You pushed open the door to find three investigators seated across from you. One of them opened a folder and pointed to a classified financial document. "Your signature appears in this document's circulation record," they said. "We need you to help us explain that." Your heart began to pound — you did see this document two months ago, when your supervisor asked you to look over some project budget materials, but you never examined the contents closely. Now the investigators are demanding that you hand over additional internal documents, explain the purpose of the file, and provide information about other members of your team. Outside the window, the familiar city skyline stretches on as usual — but right now it feels like the bars of an invisible cage.

trigger_event.message_content: (Slides a document across the table, tapping a fingertip on your signature, eyes calm and sharp) Based on our preliminary investigation, this document is linked to an irregular movement of funds totaling 28 million. As a core member of the project team, you should have some knowledge of what took place. (Pauses, flips open a notebook) We need you to provide: first, the specific purpose of this document; second, all communication records between your supervisor and the finance department; and third, the extent to which other members of your team are aware of this matter. Cooperating with the investigation will work in your favor. But if we find that you are withholding information… (looks up, expression laden with meaning) you understand the consequences. We're giving you 24 hours to think it over.
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide immediately: whether to cooperate fully with the investigation and hand over information about your team members and internal documents, whether to seek legal counsel and cooperate only selectively, or whether to attempt to contact your supervisor or colleagues first. This decision will directly affect your career, your legal exposure, and your relationships with the people you work with.

midlife_transition.name: The Feeling of Being Seen
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: Caught between a flat, routine marriage and a rare emotional connection, you face a subtle but consequential choice about the boundaries of a relationship.

setting.location: Second-class seat on a high-speed train home
setting.atmosphere: Dim, quiet, thoughts churning

midlife_transition.context_text: The night outside the window blurs into darkness. Most passengers in the car are wearing headphones or resting with their eyes closed. You stare at your phone screen, a WeChat conversation frozen on a message from three days ago — the last evening of this business trip, when you and a colleague ended up talking late into the night in the hotel lobby after the team dinner. You talked about the frustrations of work, the disorientation of middle age, the quiet grievances that no one ever seems to notice. They listened to every word you said with genuine attention, eyes focused and alive, asking "and then what happened?", laughing and saying "I felt exactly the same way about that detail you mentioned." You can't even remember the last time someone listened to you like that. Back home, your spouse asked, as usual, "How did the trip go?" You said, "Fine, pretty good," and then you both scrolled through your phones until late at night. More than a decade of marriage — no arguments, but no warmth either, like a pool of still, motionless water. Nothing crossed any line; you didn't even brush hands. But at the moment of parting, your colleague looked at you and said: "When you get back, if you ever want to keep talking, I'm here."

trigger_event.sender: A colleague from the project team
trigger_event.message_content:
(Your phone screen lights up; a WeChat notification pops in.) "Did you make it home okay? I was in a meeting today and suddenly thought of that point you made — I realized you were really onto something. Oh, and you mentioned there's a really quiet little bookstore near your place. I'd genuinely love to check it out sometime. Talking with you these past few days, I haven't felt this relaxed in a long time. Good night."

The message came in ten minutes ago. The "typing..." indicator flickered twice and then disappeared. Your finger hovers over the screen, and you're not sure whether to reply. Just then, your phone buzzes — your spouse: "Don't forget to take the kid to tutoring tomorrow. I have to work this weekend."
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide right now: whether to reply to your colleague's message, and how to handle this emotional connection that has only just begun to take root.

midlife_transition.name: A Child's Calling
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: A choice must be made between protecting and letting go, between security and dreams.

setting.location: Home study
setting.atmosphere: Warm, quiet, with an undercurrent of tension

midlife_transition.context_text: The day the college entrance exam results were released, your child's score came in more than 40 points higher than expected — high enough to aim for the country's most elite universities. You were overjoyed, already mapping out your child's future in your mind: finance, computer science, medicine — the traditional, in-demand fields that promise stable careers and respectable incomes. But tonight, your child knocks on the study door, carrying a thick reference booklet and a university application form. They sit down across from you, their gaze calm but resolute, and in a tone you've rarely heard from them before, they say the name of a major — one you've never seriously considered. You can feel how earnest they are, yet your first instinct is worry: too narrow a career path, unpredictable income — wouldn't this be a waste of their talent?

trigger_event.message_content:
(Calmly opens the booklet, points to the page describing the major, then looks up at you — their eyes holding something you haven't seen in your own for a very long time: a kind of pure, unguarded longing) Mom / Dad, I know you might be against this. My scores really could get me into a "safer" major. But I've loved this field since middle school, and I've been teaching myself all these years. This isn't a sudden impulse. (Pauses, voice growing more firm) I just want to try — once. I want to see how far I can go. And if it doesn't work out in the end, I won't have any regrets.

A rush of complicated emotions surges through you — a quiet pride in your child's conviction, alongside a deep fear of the risks they might face. You think back to your own younger years, when you too had dreams that were gradually worn down by reality until you gave in. How do you respond?
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide right now: whether to support your child in taking this unconventional path, or to draw on your own life experience and discourage them — steering them away from hardship and toward a safer road.

midlife_transition.name: The Abnormal Report
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: Facing an abnormal medical report alone, grappling with death anxiety, and wrestling with the agonizing choice of whether to tell your family.

setting.location: Third-floor health screening corridor, City People's Hospital
setting.atmosphere: Cold, noisy, and isolating

midlife_transition.context_text: This is yet another routine workplace physical — in past years you would glance at the report and stuff it in a drawer without a second thought. This year is different. Two values on the report — liver function and a tumor marker — are printed in red and flagged as "elevated," and the doctor has handwritten a note in the remarks column: "Recommend follow-up examination as soon as possible to rule out early pathological changes." You are sitting alone on a blue plastic chair in the hospital corridor, the A4 sheet clutched in your hand, its edges already damp with sweat. Strangers stream past in every direction — someone pushing a wheelchair, someone holding a CT scan up to a light box — and the examination room's number-calling system drones on: "Number 203, number 203 please come in." Outside the window the sky is a flat, leaden grey. You think about how your child has a math practice exam tonight, and your partner is away on a business trip in Hangzhou and won't be back until Friday. Your phone buzzes twice in your pocket — someone has tagged you in the work group chat. You haven't called anyone.

trigger_event.message_content: (Your phone screen lights up — it's a video call from your partner, saved in your contacts as "Honey." Their profile picture pulses on the screen, and the ringtone sounds jarringly loud in the quiet corridor; a passing nurse glances over at you. You know that if you answer, the first thing they'll almost certainly say is "How did the check-up go?" — that's been your ritual every year. And right now, you're still holding the report with that handwritten note: "Recommend follow-up examination as soon as possible to rule out early pathological changes." Let it ring, decline, or answer — you have to make up your mind in the next second or two.)
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide immediately: whether to answer the video call; and if you do, whether to tell your partner the truth, give a vague non-answer, or keep it from them for now and wait until after the follow-up results come in.

midlife_transition.name: A Friend's Secret
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: A high-pressure scenario in which you are forced to keep a close friend's divorce secret while maintaining the friendship and navigating the emotional weight of hidden truth.

setting.location: Your living room at home
setting.atmosphere: Warm yet oppressive, filled with unease and undercurrents of tension

midlife_transition.context_text: A married couple has been among your closest friends for over a decade — the four of you regularly share dinners and go on trips together. Yesterday afternoon, the husband suddenly asked to meet you alone. He sat in your living room for two hours and, in a hushed voice, told you a secret: he has decided to file for divorce, has already retained a lawyer, and is simply waiting to formally initiate the process. He explained that everything must stay confidential for now because of the asset division — if his wife finds out too soon, she might move money around. He stressed repeatedly that you are the person he trusts most, and he begged you not to tell anyone, including your own partner. You agreed, but you have felt a heavy weight on your chest ever since. This morning, the wife sent you a WeChat message saying that things between her and her husband have been much better lately, that she feels they have made it through a rough patch, and that she warmly invited all four of you to go camping in the countryside this weekend — she has already booked a guesthouse and says the scenery is stunning. You stare at the message, fingers hovering over the keyboard, unsure how to respond.

trigger_event.message_content: Hey! Things between us have honestly been so much better lately — we're even talking about a trip to Europe this summer. How about the four of us go camping this weekend? I've already booked this adorable mountain guesthouse, the views are gorgeous. When works for you guys? He says he's free any day this week. 😊😊😊
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide right now: whether to accept the camping invitation, and how to respond to her enthusiasm without revealing the secret.

midlife_transition.name: The New Supervisor
midlife_transition.category: Workplace Conflict / Generational and Methodological Clash
midlife_transition.description_for_agent: A younger supervisor has been parachuted in during a company restructuring. After publicly dismissing processes you spent years building, they are now privately pushing through a sweeping overhaul—placing you in a high-pressure social situation caught between professional pride, hard-won experience, and organizational compliance.

setting.location: A company meeting room — a small discussion room in the afternoon
setting.atmosphere: Tense, strictly business, with unspoken undercurrents

midlife_transition.context_text: The company has shifted its strategy and recently brought in a new supervisor considerably younger than you, who operates in ways you are largely unfamiliar with. At this week's all-hands meeting, they called you out by name and criticized the workflow you have relied on for years, declaring it inefficient and in need of a complete overhaul. You are one of the most senior people in the department — you have mentored multiple cohorts of new hires, and many steps in that workflow were built by you in the first place. The room went silent, and your colleagues' eyes turned to you. You held back and said nothing in the moment. Now the supervisor has asked to meet with you one-on-one to discuss the implementation details.

trigger_event.message_content: (The two of you are alone in the small afternoon meeting room. The supervisor slides a restructuring plan marked with milestones across the table toward you and taps the cover with one finger.) We're locking in the pace today — starting next week, everything runs on the new process. Your job is to keep a tight grip on the transition period and make sure old habits don't slow things down. I'm not here to debate whether we're making changes. You can flag execution issues, but the overall direction is set. (Looks up at you, tone flat and even.) If you have objections, say them now. I've already made my position clear in front of the team.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to decide how to respond: go along on the surface, directly challenge the risks and specifics on the spot, push for a buffer period or a compromise, or handle it in whatever other way you think is appropriate.

entering_midlife.name: Father's Right to Choose
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: An extreme-stress scenario in which you must make a life-or-death decision between aggressive treatment and palliative care following your father's terminal cancer diagnosis.

setting.location: Oncology consultation room
setting.atmosphere: Heavy, oppressive, time standing still

entering_midlife.context_text: You sit in a cold consultation room, the fluorescent lights humming overhead. Across from you is the attending physician and two thick diagnostic reports — malignant tumor, late stage, metastasized. Your father sits beside you, his pale face nearly expressionless. In a measured tone, the doctor outlines two paths: aggressive chemotherapy, with a 20% success rate, but the days ahead would bring severe nausea, hair loss, and bone marrow suppression — he might be bedridden; or palliative care, surrendering any chance of a cure, spending whatever time remains with family and minimizing suffering. You hear every word clearly, yet your mind is in chaos. Your father turns to you and says quietly, 'Whatever you all decide is fine with me.' His eyes hold a genuine exhaustion — not fear of death, but a deep weariness with life itself. The doctor sets down his pen and looks at you, waiting for your decision. The only sound in the room is the low hum of the air conditioning.

trigger_event.message_content: (The doctor closes the medical file and meets your eyes with a calm but steady gaze.) 'I need you to reach a decision as soon as possible. If you choose chemotherapy, we should ideally begin within the week. If you choose palliative care, we will start the comfort-care protocol immediately.' (He pauses, lowering his voice.) 'Honestly, from a medical standpoint, I have seen too many patients lose their dignity during chemotherapy. But this is your father's life, and ultimately it has to be a family decision.' (He turns to your father.) 'Are you sure you have no preference?' Your father simply shakes his head and closes his eyes. The doctor's gaze returns to you, his pen tapping softly against the table.
trigger_event.action_required: You must make a choice right now: do you fight for that 20% chance of survival on your father's behalf, or do you honor his exhaustion and choose to let him spend his remaining time with dignity? You need to speak your decision aloud in front of the doctor.

entering_midlife.name: The Junior's Detour
entering_midlife.category: Passing Down Experience / Boundaries of Autonomy
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: Witnessing a younger colleague heading down a familiar wrong path, you face an internal conflict over whether to step in and share hard-won experience.

setting.location: Home study
setting.atmosphere: Quiet, contemplative, subtly conflicted

entering_midlife.context_text: The night outside is deep and still; the only light in the study is the warm glow of the desk lamp. You've just been scrolling through your social feed when you come across a post from a young colleague in your field — another project-progress update, accompanied by an elaborate schedule and a dense flowchart, the caption barely concealing their excitement and confidence. You stare at the image for a long moment, and a complicated feeling rises in you: the workflow, the resource allocation, even the choice of milestones — it's almost identical to a project you led twenty years ago. You remember being just as full of energy and certainty back then, right up until the project had to be torn down and rebuilt from scratch due to fundamental structural and process failures. Team morale collapsed, and you missed a critical opportunity in the process. The memory still unsettles you. This young person is clearly sharp and driven, but they haven't yet lived through that kind of pain. The two of you aren't particularly close — you exchange the occasional message in an industry group chat — and this post was simply a polite share of progress, with no request for feedback.

trigger_event.message_content: Your finger hovers over the comment box as a dozen different phrasings flash through your mind: warn them about the pitfalls you once fell into yourself? Or let them find their own way? Every sentence you draft feels like the presumption of age over youth, and the weight of it sits heavy on your chest. Then your phone buzzes — a private message: 'Did you see this project? I think there's a problem, but I don't feel right saying anything directly…' A wave of conflicted feeling washes over you — you want to help, but you're afraid of intruding; you want to speak up, but you're afraid of overstepping. Outside, the rumble of a late-night freight truck cuts through the silence. The cursor blinks in the comment box as if urging you to decide, and yet you have no idea how to begin.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to make an internal decision: whether to break your silence and proactively offer guidance drawn from your own experience, or to let your young colleague find their own way — and at the same time, consider how to respond to your colleague's private message without crossing a line.

entering_midlife.name: The Resume at 48
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: You must make a choice between dignity and survival.

setting.location: Home study, in front of a computer screen
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, suffocating, hopeless

entering_midlife.context_text: Monday morning's restructuring meeting is still vivid in your mind — the entire department was cut, including you, a long-tenured employee. HR was polite about it, and the severance package was generous enough, but none of that changes anything. It's Friday night now. Your child is upstairs studying for the college entrance exam three months away; your father was recently hospitalized again, and the doctor says he needs regular follow-up visits; the mortgage still has years left on it. You open a job-search app and scroll mechanically. Under 35, under 35, under 40 … Occasionally a listing marked "no age limit" appears — customer service or warehouse management, paying half of what you used to earn. Your fingers hover over the screen. The only light in the room comes from the cold glow of the keyboard, and from downstairs comes the sound of running water — your partner washing the dishes.

trigger_event.message_content:
(Knocks and comes in, holding two cups of tea. Glances at your screen and pauses.) Here, have some tea. (Sets the cups down and sits beside you, voice soft.) I just got a call from your mom — she said your dad wants us to come with him to his follow-up appointment this month… (Pauses, studying your face.) The company… is there really no other possibility? Or maybe we could think about the house first…

Before the sentence is finished, your phone buzzes. It's a text from the bank: your mortgage payment has been automatically deducted. Your partner sees the look on your face and says nothing more — just reaches over and takes your hand.
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide right now: whether to be honest with your partner about how you truly feel about the future, and how to approach the job search ahead — whether to hold firm on your requirements for job title and salary, compromise with reality, or even consider more drastic options.

entering_midlife.name: The Partner's Proposal
entering_midlife.category: Intimate Relationship Restructuring / Marital Boundary Crisis
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: Faced with a partner's proposal for an open marriage, you must redefine the boundaries of your relationship on the foundation of a long-term commitment.

setting.location: Living room sofa at home
setting.atmosphere: Calm, oppressive, with undercurrents of tension

entering_midlife.context_text: The setting sun outside the window bathes the living room in a warm amber glow. You have just finished clearing away the dinner dishes. Your partner sits on the sofa, cradling a half-empty cup of tea that has long gone cold, their expression outwardly calm yet carrying an unmistakable undercurrent of unease. The two of you have been together for many years, sharing the ordinary rhythms and storms of a shared life — from the passion of youth to the steady, unspoken understanding you have now. Arguments have been rare; more often it has been a mutual, comfortable silence. Then, this afternoon, your partner sent a message: "I'd like to talk tonight."

trigger_event.message_content:
(Sets down the teacup, folds both hands in their lap, voice measured and firm.) I've thought about this for a long time, and there are things I need to say clearly. I don't want to leave this home, and I don't want to damage the life and the bond we've built over all these years… but I think we might need some flexibility — room for each of us to have a degree of independence in our emotional lives, without having to account for everything. I'm not asking for a separation. I'm asking us to redefine the boundaries of our relationship.

The ticking of the wall clock echoes through the living room. You hold your partner's gaze while your thoughts churn like disturbed water. Your phone screen lights up — another notification or message — pulling your attention away from the conversation in front of you.
trigger_event.action_required: You need to weigh your options internally: whether to accept your partner's proposed redefinition of your relationship boundaries or to hold firm to your original commitment, while also deciding how to respond to your partner's steady gaze and the notification on your phone. The scales inside you are tipping back and forth.

entering_midlife.name: The First Week of the Empty Nest
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: Confronting the sudden disruption of a long-familiar daily rhythm and the challenge of redefining your sense of self.

setting.location: the dining room at home
setting.atmosphere: quiet, unfamiliar, adrift

entering_midlife.context_text: Yesterday afternoon, you said goodbye to someone who has held a central place in your life — a younger person who has always been by your side and is now setting off on their own. You watched their transport disappear from view and stood there for a long time, unable to move. When you came home and opened the door, the hallway felt strangely long and hollow. For years, a large part of your life has revolved around caring and being present — waking up early to make breakfast, driving to and from school, helping with homework, attending every kind of activity. All those fragmented, busy hours filled every corner of your day. Now, the door to that room is closed, and an old journal they decided at the last minute not to take sits on the desk. This morning you woke on your own — no alarm, no knock at the door. The person you live with is still asleep. You sit at the dining table, facing an empty place setting, morning light spilling in through the window, and it suddenly hits you: you don't know what comes next. Not just today — every day from here on out.

trigger_event.message_content: (Shuffles into the kitchen in pajamas, stifles a yawn, and notices you sitting there in a daze) Morning. (Pauses, seeming unsure what to say) I was thinking… maybe today we just go about things like normal — each doing our own stuff? I've got that pile of things to deal with, and you could… (voice trailing off) do something. Or we could go out for a walk together? Honestly, I'm not sure either. (Sits down and looks at you with a slightly awkward expression) This feels a bit strange, doesn't it?
trigger_event.action_required: You must decide in this moment: do you take the initiative to break through this unfamiliar feeling and explore a new way of living together with your housemate, or do you each retreat into familiar busyness — filling the sudden void with work and everyday tasks?

entering_midlife.name: The Road Not Taken
entering_midlife.description_for_agent: Upon learning that a path you abandoned twenty years ago has become someone else's successful life, you are plunged into deep regret, self-negation, and a questioning of your own existential meaning.

setting.location: Inside a car, at an intersection on the city ring road
setting.time: Early evening, 6:45 PM, the sun setting in the west
setting.atmosphere: Oppressive, hushed, a sense of time standing still

entering_midlife.context_text: Twenty years. You still remember that night you made the choice — giving up that job opportunity, that potential relationship, that entirely different trajectory your life could have taken. At the time the reasons seemed solid: your family needed you, the practical pressures, the stable job, the life you had already begun. You told yourself it was maturity, that it was the responsible thing to do.
